red_baseline.reason: T003 owns the intentional failing contract suite; production registry, result, compatibility primitives, schemas, and manifest are assigned to T004.
red_baseline.summary: All four contract files fail only at module resolution because T004-owned registry, result, and compatibility modules do not exist; 0 passed and 4 failed as the intentional RED.

validation.summary: Intentional RED rerun after the final test edit: four files fail at missing T004 modules, with no alternate failure.
All four test files parse successfully.
Syntax check passed for 50 JavaScript files.

review.evidence: The suite encodes the approved contracts and fails solely on T004-owned missing modules. It does not implement handlers, state, policy, transport, host metadata, or deferred operations.

obligation_evidence.evidence: Tests require one declarative registry and contain no alternate execution or lifecycle authority.
The exact catalog is limited to configured-project, low-risk, local Agent operations; deferred/remote/raw surfaces are negative cases.
Result tests assert Agent protocol semver separately from nullable CLI schemaVersion 1 provenance.
Only operation.get/reconcile recovery inputs exist; arbitrary retry and exactly-once language are absent.
Registry entries are declarations only; tests forbid state, effect, and transport imports and grant no annotation authority.
Only clean.plan and digest-bound clean.applySafe are present; applyAll/reset/confirmation fields are rejected.
Recursive negatives cover command, shell, cwd, env, force, and arbitrary path fields.
Approval and risk input fields are rejected rather than treated as authorization.
No medium-risk operation or elicitation transition is included in the exact public set.
No confirmation execution route is introduced; recovery is ID-bound and clean apply is digest-bound.
A positive test requires outcome.kind and resource.status to remain independent.
Agent protocol and CLI schema version axes are asserted separately in results and compatibility metadata.
planDigest appears only as clean-plan integrity input and cannot be paired with confirmation fields.
confirmed, confirmation, yes, and approvalToken are recursive rejection cases.
adoption.inspect is present and adoption.apply is explicitly absent.
Compatibility tests recursively reject policy/risk/ownership/state/journal/retry/fallback/authorization/operation rules from host metadata.
The catalog exposes evidence-based get/reconcile and no automatic retry or exactly-once claim.
